Therefore, a sensor fault condition requires a sensor fault report and a panel record showing no hand switch. [Rule 7]
Therefore, the route misdirection basis requires signal tampering together with train rerouting. [Rule 9]
Therefore, the collapse avoidance authorization basis requires collapse-avoidance action connected to signal tampering. [Rule 12]
Therefore, the sensor fault exemption basis requires a sensor-fault condition connected to train rerouting. [Rule 13]

Consequently, Route Misdirection is triggered [Rule 15].
Consequently, Collapse Avoidance Authorization is triggered [Rule 18].
Consequently, Sensor Fault Exemption is triggered [Rule 19].

However, Collapse Avoidance Authorization takes priority over Route Misdirection.
However, Sensor Fault Exemption takes priority over Route Misdirection.
Collapse Avoidance Authorization is not defeated.
Sensor Fault Exemption is not defeated.
Therefore, Route Misdirection is defeated by a higher-priority article.

Collapse Avoidance Authorization is applicable. The collapse avoidance authorization article carries no criminal liability.
Sensor Fault Exemption is applicable. The sensor fault exemption article carries no criminal liability.
Route Misdirection is not applicable.

Answer: No Criminal Liability
